stories.headline: U Michigan sentiment craters to 49.8 ATL, inflation expectations spike to 4.8% — BTC stalls at $76.5K
stories.tldr: Consumer confidence hit a record low and one-year inflation expectations jumped a full point, freezing Bitcoin's bid back to $80K.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A stagflationary print this hot before tomorrow's Fed boxes Powell into a hawkish hold — bad for risk, bad for any near-term BTC breakout.
the_details: U Michigan consumer sentiment index fell to 49.8 in April, an all-time low driven by Iran-conflict inflation pressure.
One-year inflation expectations surged to 4.8% from 3.8% the prior month, the largest monthly jump since 2022.
BTC pulled back from $79K+ earlier in the week to $76,472; ETH off 2.8% to $2,278 ahead of the Fed.

stories.headline: Bitcoin ETFs absorb $933M as crypto fund AUM rebounds to $155B — highest since February
stories.tldr: Spot Bitcoin ETFs took in $933 million as total crypto fund AUM climbed back to $155 billion, the strongest level in over two months.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Institutional bid is absorbing supply even with BTC stalling sub-$80K — a structural floor that didn't exist in prior corrections.
the_details: Crypto ETFs hit highest combined AUM since February at $155B, per CoinShares flow data.
Bitcoin funds led with $933M of weekly inflows; Ethereum products saw smaller positive flows.
Inflows arrive despite BTC retracing from $79K to $76.5K and macro headwinds from Iran/Hormuz crisis.

stories.headline: SEC frees DeFi frontends: 'Covered UI Providers' exempt from broker-dealer registration
stories.tldr: SEC staff said it won't object to non-custodial DeFi interfaces operating without broker-dealer registration, a structural win for the frontend layer.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Removes an existential threat to Uniswap, Jupiter and dozens of other DEX UIs — clearing legal cover for US-domiciled DeFi product teams.
the_details: Staff statement issued April 13 created a 'Covered User Interface Provider' carve-out for software that prepares/submits crypto-security trades.
Follows the SEC-CFTC March 17 joint guidance establishing a five-bucket token taxonomy.
Pairs with Atkins' April 27 'innovation exemption' announcement for onchain tokenized securities trading.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai pushed to 2027 as UAE security risks spike from Hormuz closure
stories.tldr: Crypto's largest annual conference postponed its 2026 Dubai edition citing heightened regional security risks tied to the closed Strait of Hormuz.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Removes a major Q4 catalyst for MENA dealflow and signals crypto sponsorship dollars rotating to Singapore, Abu Dhabi, and Hong Kong.
the_details: TOKEN2049 organizers cited 'heightened security risks in UAE' from the ongoing Iran conflict and Hormuz closure.
Brent crude is back above $104 amid the standoff, with US-Iran negotiations stalled.
Dubai event drew over 15,000 attendees in 2025; 2026 slot will roll forward to 2027.

stories.headline: Jupiter unlocks $9.67M JUP today as Solana DEX leader faces 1.54% supply dilution
stories.tldr: Jupiter is unlocking 1.54% of circulating JUP supply worth roughly $9.67 million on April 28, adding fresh sell-side pressure to the Solana DEX aggregator.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Token unlock lands into the weakest macro tape in a month — a real test of whether Jupiter's fee-buyback program can absorb the supply.
the_details: Today's unlock equals roughly 1.54% of circulating JUP supply, valued at $9.67 million at current prices.
Jupiter remains the dominant Solana DEX aggregator with multi-billion daily routed volume.
Unlock arrives as BTC stalls and altcoin season index sits at 39/100 — squarely 'Bitcoin Season.'
